title: SOURCE NEEMISHA BANSAL 120 GRAMMAR RULES TOPIC ERROR QU

questions.question: [RULE 01: EACH VS EVERY]
options: Every of the five candidates who appeared for the interview was exceptionally talented in their respective fields.
questions.explanation: 'Every' is only an adjective and cannot be used as a pronoun; 'Each' must be used here. 'Every' केवल adjective है, pronoun नहीं; यहाँ 'Each' का प्रयोग होगा।

questions.question: He held a heavy leather briefcase in every hand while boarding the crowded train yesterday.
questions.explanation: 'Every' is used for more than two; for two hands, 'each' is correct. 'Every' दो से अधिक के लिए होता है; दो हाथों के लिए 'each' सही है।

questions.question: The company ensured that every reasons for the delay were documented and presented to the board.
questions.explanation: 'Every' is always followed by a singular noun; 'reason' should be used. 'Every' के बाद हमेशा singular noun आता है; 'reason' का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: Each of the students have been assigned a unique mentor to guide them through the research project.
questions.explanation: 'Each' takes a singular verb regardless of the plural noun following it. 'Each' के साथ हमेशा singular verb आती है, चाहे उसके बाद plural noun हो।

questions.question: They have each reason to celebrate after winning the prestigious national championship this year.
questions.explanation: 'Every' is used with abstract nouns like 'reason' for emphasis, not 'each'. 'Reason' जैसे abstract nouns के साथ 'every' का प्रयोग होता है, 'each' का नहीं।

questions.question: Every soldier in the battalion were ready to sacrifice their lives for the safety of the nation.
questions.explanation: 'Every' is followed by a singular verb 'was'. 'Every' के साथ singular verb 'was' का प्रयोग होना चाहिए।

questions.question: In the meeting, each members was given exactly three minutes to express their concerns regarding the policy.
questions.explanation: 'Each' as an adjective is followed by a singular noun 'member'. 'Each' जब adjective हो तो उसके बाद singular noun 'member' आता है।

questions.question: Both the sisters were invited, but every girl decided to stay at home and study.
questions.explanation: 'Every' cannot be used for exactly two; 'each' or 'both' is appropriate. 'Every' का प्रयोग दो के लिए नहीं होता; 'each' का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: Each of the two paths lead to the same destination, though one is significantly steeper than the other.
questions.explanation: 'Each' requires a singular verb 'leads'. 'Each' के साथ singular verb 'leads' आनी चाहिए।

questions.question: The teacher gave every of the students a different assignment to prevent any chance of plagiarism.
questions.explanation: 'Every' cannot be followed by 'of'; 'Each of' is the correct structure. 'Every' के बाद 'of' नहीं आता; 'Each of' सही structure है।

questions.question: [RULE 02: BOTH AND NOT]
options: Both of the leading candidates are not suitable for the position of the Managing Director.
questions.explanation: 'Both' and 'not' cannot be used together; use 'Neither...is'. 'Both' और 'not' साथ नहीं आते; 'Neither...is' का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: This is a complex legal battle which both parties cannot afford to lose at this stage.
questions.explanation: Replace 'both...cannot' with 'neither party can'. 'Both...cannot' की जगह 'neither party can' का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: Both Ram and Shyam did not attend the seminar held at the university yesterday.
questions.explanation: Use 'Neither Ram nor Shyam attended'. 'Both...not' के स्थान पर 'Neither Ram nor Shyam attended' का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: It was a rare situation where both sides were not willing to compromise on their demands.
questions.explanation: Use 'neither side was willing'. 'Neither side was willing' का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: Both of my friends have not seen the latest blockbuster movie yet.
questions.explanation: Change to 'Neither of my friends has seen'. 'Neither of my friends has seen' में बदलें।

questions.question: Since both of them are not coming, we should cancel the dinner reservation immediately.
questions.explanation: Use 'Neither of them is coming'. 'Neither of them is coming' सही प्रयोग है।

questions.question: The report stated that both the engines were not functioning properly during the flight.
questions.explanation: Use 'Neither of the engines was functioning'. 'Neither of the engines was functioning' का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: Both the brothers do not like to play cricket in the scorching heat of the afternoon.
questions.explanation: Replace with 'Neither of the brothers likes'. 'Neither of the brothers likes' के साथ बदलें।

questions.question: I realized that both of the solutions were not applicable to the problem I was facing.
questions.explanation: Use 'Neither of the solutions was'. 'Neither of the solutions was' का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: Both the participants have not submitted their entry forms before the deadline.
questions.explanation: Use 'Neither of the participants has submitted'. 'Neither of the participants has submitted' का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: [RULE 03: RELATIVE PRONOUNS]
options: This is the same man who met me in the library last week and discussed the project.
questions.explanation: 'The same' is followed by 'that', not 'who'. 'The same' के बाद 'that' आता है, 'who' नहीं।

questions.question: He spent the little money which he had saved for his daughter's higher education.
questions.explanation: 'The little' is followed by 'that', not 'which'. 'The little' के बाद 'that' आता है, 'which' नहीं।

questions.question: The dog who was barking at the stranger belongs to the new neighbor.
questions.explanation: For animals in general, use 'which' or 'that', not 'who'. जानवरों के लिए 'which' या 'that' का प्रयोग करें, 'who' का नहीं।

questions.question: Give the award to whomever is found to be the most deserving by the jury members.
questions.explanation: 'Whoever' is needed as the subject for the verb 'is'. Verb 'is' के subject के रूप में 'Whoever' की आवश्यकता है।

questions.question: Whomever wins the election will have to face significant economic challenges.
questions.explanation: 'Whoever' is the subjective pronoun required here for 'wins'. 'Wins' के लिए यहाँ subjective pronoun 'Whoever' चाहिए।

questions.question: This is the best which we can do under the current circumstances.
questions.explanation: Superlative degrees are followed by 'that'. Superlative degree के बाद 'that' का प्रयोग होता है।

questions.question: The musician which won the Grammy last night is originally from a small village in India.
questions.explanation: Use 'who' or 'that' for people; 'which' is for things/animals. व्यक्तियों के लिए 'who' या 'that' का प्रयोग करें; 'which' निर्जीव/जानवरों के लिए है।

questions.question: Our cat Luna, that was sleeping on the sofa, got scared by the thunder.
questions.explanation: For pets mentioned by name, use 'who'. नाम वाले पालतू जानवरों के लिए 'who' का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: Anything which you say will be used against you in the court of law.
questions.explanation: 'Anything' is followed by 'that', not 'which'. 'Anything' के बाद 'that' आता है, 'which' नहीं।

questions.question: Please invite whoever you want to the party, as long as they are respectful.
questions.explanation: 'Whomever' is the objective pronoun required for 'you want (him)'. 'You want' के object के रूप में 'Whomever' चाहिए।

questions.question: [RULE 04: CONDITIONAL SENTENCES]
options: If I will study hard, I will definitely pass the competitive examination this year.
questions.explanation: In Type I conditional, simple present is used in the if-clause. Type I conditional में if-clause में simple present का प्रयोग होता है।

questions.question: If she had invited me properly, I would attend the wedding ceremony last night.
questions.explanation: For Type III (past), use 'would have attended'. Type III के लिए 'would have attended' का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: If I were the Prime Minister, I will eliminate poverty from the country.
questions.explanation: Type II conditional uses 'would + V1'. Type II conditional में 'would + V1' का प्रयोग होता है।

questions.question: Had you obeyed the traffic rules, the accident would have been averted easily.
questions.explanation: This is a correct Type III conditional structure. यह एक सही Type III conditional structure है।

questions.question: If you heat ice, it will turn into water eventually.
questions.explanation: For universal truths, use simple present in both clauses: 'turns'. सार्वभौमिक सत्य के लिए दोनों clauses में simple present 'turns' का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: If he worked harder, he will surely get a promotion in the next quarter.
questions.explanation: Type II (Simple Past) requires 'would get'. Type II के लिए 'would get' की आवश्यकता है।

questions.question: If the weather is good tomorrow, we would go for a picnic in the park.
questions.explanation: Type I requires 'will go' in the main clause. Type I में main clause में 'will go' होना चाहिए।

questions.question: If I had known about your arrival, I would have come to the station to pick you up.
questions.explanation: Correct Type III conditional form. यह Type III conditional का सही रूप है।

questions.question: If water reaches 100 degrees Celsius, it would boil.
questions.explanation: Universal truth takes simple present: 'boils'. सार्वभौमिक सत्य simple present 'boils' लेता है।

questions.question: Unless you do not work hard, you will not be able to achieve your goals.
questions.explanation: 'Unless' is already negative; omit 'do not'. 'Unless' पहले से ही नकारात्मक है; 'do not' हटा दें।

questions.question: [RULE 05: ONE OF THE]
options: One of the most talented student in the class has been selected for the scholarship.
questions.explanation: 'One of the' is always followed by a plural noun: 'students'. 'One of the' के बाद हमेशा plural noun 'students' आता है।

questions.question: She is one of the authors who has written extensively on the subject of climate change.
questions.explanation: Relative pronoun 'who' refers to 'authors', so use plural verb 'have'. 'Who' का संबंध 'authors' से है, इसलिए plural verb 'have' का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: One of the books that is lying on the table belongs to the teacher.
questions.explanation: Relative pronoun 'that' refers to 'books', so use plural verb 'are'. 'That' का संबंध 'books' से है, इसलिए plural verb 'are' का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: This is one of the only movies that have won ten Academy Awards.
questions.explanation: With 'only one of', the verb is always singular: 'has'. 'Only one of' के साथ verb हमेशा singular 'has' होती है।

questions.question: One of my friends are going to London for higher studies next month.
questions.explanation: If 'one of the' is the subject, the verb is singular: 'is'. अगर 'one of the' subject है, तो verb singular 'is' होगी।

questions.question: He is one of those players who never gives up until the very last moment.
questions.explanation: 'Who' refers to 'players', so use plural verb 'give up'. 'Who' का संबंध 'players' से है, इसलिए plural verb 'give up' का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: That is one of the birds that flies over my house every morning.
questions.explanation: 'That' refers to 'birds', so use plural verb 'fly'. 'That' का संबंध 'birds' से है, इसलिए plural verb 'fly' का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: One of the problems that was discussed in the meeting remains unresolved.
questions.explanation: Relative pronoun 'that' refers to 'problems', so use plural verb 'were'. 'That' का संबंध 'problems' से है, इसलिए 'were' का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: She is one of the mothers who is working hard to provide a better life for her children.
questions.explanation: 'Who' refers to 'mothers', so use plural verb 'are'. 'Who' का संबंध 'mothers' से है, इसलिए plural verb 'are' का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: One of the reasons for his failure are his lack of discipline and focus.
questions.explanation: The subject is 'One', so the verb must be singular: 'is'. Subject 'One' है, इसलिए verb singular 'is' होनी चाहिए।
